summary:
Highlights

- Verifier verified Lemmas A–D and appended them to `proofs.md`. Lemma 3 fixes cross-witness slope interactions onto affine 1D lines (e.g. $C_{ij}=(h_i-h_j)/(\lambda_i-\lambda_j)$).
- Affine-line collapse: identical cross-slopes form collinear families; affine multiplicity bounds yield group size $|\Gamma|\le 3$.
- Hard-regime obstruction: for $|R(\phi)|>9n'/32$ the opposite-branch sets satisfy $|F_i|>31n'/64\approx0.484n'$, which is below the $0.5n'$ list-decoding requirement, so standard per-function list-decoding cannot resolve the hard case.
- Cocycle limitation: the 3-witness cocycle $Q_k\neq 0$ vanishes on $Z=(E_1\cap E_2\cap E_k)\cup(F_1\cap F_2\cap F_k)$, but averaging shows $\mathbb{E}[|Z|]\approx\frac{m-4}{4(m-1)}n'<n'/4=k'$, so no single triple can be guaranteed to force $Q_k\equiv 0$.
- Recommended pivot: treat all witnesses jointly. Stack evaluations into an $m\times n'$ matrix $M_{ij}=h_i(x)$: columns are (per-coordinate) nearly affine lines while rows lie in $RS[H^2,k']$ (row-rank $\le k'$). Seek global interleaved-RS / multiplicity-decoding nullspace relations that block independent assignments of the $F_i$ error blocks.

Open questions / TODOs

1. Formulate a concrete global nullspace / interleaved-RS statement (polynomial-matrix or multiplicity relation) that must vanish across all columns and contradict free packing of the $F_i$.
2. Quantify minimal $m$ and derive explicit inequalities that turn per-coordinate line-isolation plus row-rank $\le k'$ into a contradiction (compute the numeric threshold needed to beat the $0.484n'$ gap).

Citations: `proofs.md`, `notes.md`, verifier feedback (appended to `proofs.md`).
one_line_summary: Lemmas A–D checked; slopes collapse to affine lines ($|\Gamma|\le 3$); bottleneck $|F_i|\approx0.484n'<0.5n'$; pivot to global interleaved-RS.
